front: What is a class in OOP?
back: A blueprint for creating objects that defines their attributes (data) and methods (behavior).
Constructor that initializes new instances of a class, setting up initial attributes.
Refers to the current instance and must be first parameter in all methods.
Ability for a class to inherit attributes and methods from a parent class.
Allows methods to be accessed as attributes (without calling them as functions).

front: What is the difference between __str__ and __repr__?
back: __str__ is user-friendly representation, __repr__ is official/technical representation.
